div.head:
No. 552.
Mr. Blaine to Mr. Christiancy.
div.p: Sir: In your last dispatch, No.—, you informed this Department that the Chilian Government refused absolutely to recognize General Piérola as representing the civil authority in Peru, and that Señor Calderon was at the head of a provisional government.

If the Calderon government is supported by the character and intelligence of Peru and is really endeavoring to restore constitutional government with a view both to order within and negotiation with Chili for peace, you may recognize it as the existing provisional government, and render what aid you can by advice and good offices to that end.
Mr. Elmore has been received by me as the confidential agent of such provisional government.
